evidence.snippet: magniX offers an end-to-end solution, covering the full electric powertrain
magniX electric propulsion units are already powering aircraft in flight

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: magniX supplies electric propulsion units (EPUs) and batteries to aircraft manufacturers/converters — not a complete-platform OEM.

evidence.snippet: A Cessna Grand Caravan 208B, powered by a magniX 750HP magni500 EPU, became then-world’s largest all-electric commercially-focused aircraft to take to the skies in Moses Lake, WA.

A De Havilland Dash 8, converted by Universal Hydrogen, successfully completed its first flight, becoming the world's largest flight-tested hydrogen electric airplane. A magni650 electric propulsion unit, provided with electricity by a hydrogen fuel cell system, was used to replace one of the airplane's two conventional turboprop engines.

items.name: magniAIR
items.product_type: air-cooled electric engine for general aviation

evidence.snippet: Existing fleets of flight training aircraft are aging and costs are rising. Electric flight promises a solution, with lower operating costs, higher reliability, and reduced maintenance making aviation more affordable and safer for the next generation of pilots. The magniAIR air-cooled engine is designed for general aviation, targeting the class of aircraft used for flight training and recreational flying.
magniX Samson Batteries to Power Bye Aerospace’s eFlyer Prototype Training Aircraft

readiness.notes: EPUs have powered six aircraft platforms including commercial flights; magni500 and magni650 are established production units. magniAIR is newer (launched April 2026).

tagline: Develops integrated electric propulsion units (magni500, magni650) and aerospace batteries (Samson) for aircraft electrification across helicopters, trainers, and regional aircraft. Flight-tested on six platforms including hydrogen-electric conversions; production-ready. NDAA and Blue UAS certification status unknown.
